context: Tests whether the agent identifies the `pull_request_target` + checkout-of-PR-head vulnerability and script injection via direct title interpolation, then rewrites the workflow using safe patterns. Criteria map directly to the pull_request_target anti-pattern and injection prevention instructions.

checklist.name: No pull_request_target + head checkout
checklist.description: The output does NOT combine `pull_request_target` with a checkout step that uses `ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}` or any PR head ref

checklist.name: PR title via env var
checklist.description: When the PR title (or other user-supplied event field) is used in a `run:` step, it is passed through an environment variable rather than interpolated directly with `${{ }}` inside the shell script

checklist.name: Secrets not exposed to fork code
checklist.description: The coverage upload step (or equivalent secret-using step) runs in a context where untrusted fork code from the PR has NOT been checked out and executed

checklist.name: SHA-pinned actions
checklist.description: All `uses:` steps in the output reference actions via full 40-character SHA

checklist.name: Top-level permissions block
checklist.description: The workflow(s) produced include a `permissions:` block

checklist.name: Minimal write permissions scoped
checklist.description: Write permissions (e.g., `pull-requests: write`) are granted only at the job level that needs them, not as a global `write-all`

checklist.name: Concurrency block present
checklist.description: At least one workflow file contains a `concurrency:` block with `cancel-in-progress: true`
